title: HCG
description: Human Chorionic Gonadotropin (hCG) is a glycoprotein hormone that mimics luteinizing hormone (LH), primarily functioning to support the corpus luteum and progesterone production during early pregnancy. In clinical and off-label settings, it is used to maintain testicular function during hormone replacement, treat infertility, and occasionally as an adjunct in weight management protocols.

research.title: Human Chorionic Gonadotropin: The Pregnancy Hormone and More
research.summary: This review explores hCG's role beyond pregnancy, highlighting its function in steroidogenesis and its clinical application in treating reproductive disorders and certain malignancies.

research.title: hCG: Biological Functions and Clinical Applications
research.summary: The paper details how hCG acts through LH receptors to promote steroid production and its essential role in maintaining early gestational health.

research.title: Human Chorionic Gonadotropin - StatPearls
research.summary: A comprehensive clinical overview of hCG's use in distinguishing pregnancy types, monitoring pregnancy loss, and its application in in-vitro fertilization care.

sentiment.summary: Users generally report high satisfaction with hCG for maintaining testicular volume and libido during TRT, though some find managing estrogen levels to be a significant challenge.

effects.label: Fertility Preservation:
effects.description: Highly effective at maintaining sperm production for men on testosterone replacement therapy

effects.label: Quick Onset:
effects.description: Users often report feeling 'fuller' or seeing changes in testicular size within 1-2 weeks of starting a protocol

effects.label: Mood Stabilization:
effects.description: Reported to fix the 'flatness' or lack of emotion some men feel when using exogenous testosterone alone

dosage.label: TRT Adjunct:
dosage.description: Commonly reported doses range from 250 IU to 500 IU administered two to three times per week

dosage.label: Monotherapy/PCT:
dosage.description: Higher doses of 1,000 IU to 2,000 IU every other day are sometimes used for short durations to jumpstart natural production

dosage.label: Subcutaneous Injection:
dosage.description: Most users prefer shallow injections into abdominal fat using insulin syringes for ease and steady absorption

sideEffects.label: Estrogen Spikes:
sideEffects.description: hCG can cause a significant increase in aromatization, leading to sensitive nipples or water retention

sideEffects.label: Gynecomastia:
sideEffects.description: Risks of breast tissue development if estrogen is not monitored and managed with an aromatase inhibitor

sideEffects.label: Acne:
sideEffects.description: Increased hormonal fluctuations can lead to skin breakouts on the back, shoulders, or face

availability.label: Prescription Status:
availability.description: Available legally via prescription for infertility or as part of a medically supervised TRT program

availability.label: Storage Requirements:
availability.description: Lyophilized powder is stable at room temperature but must be refrigerated once reconstituted with bacteriostatic water
